title: mesua manii
intro: Mesua manii, also known as the Nagpur kino tree, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant's inner bark has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its astringent properties, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The medicinal properties of Mesua manii are said to provide various health benefits, including treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing sore throats. Herbal preparations of Mesua manii include infusions of the inner bark, which can be consumed as a tea, and decoctions of the bark, which are used to make medicinal tonics.
